profile.name: Ayush Juneja
profile.specialty: Next.js, Node.js, E-commerce Systems, Admin Dashboards, Mobile Apps, Automation
profile.availability: Open to full-time roles, freelance builds, and product collaborations
profile.intro: Full stack developer with nearly 2 years of professional experience building scalable web and mobile applications, e-commerce platforms, and business-focused digital systems.
profile.shortBio: Focused on end-to-end product development, backend architecture, admin systems, analytics, operational workflows, and responsive user experiences.
profile.story: I have worked across startup environments where product speed, scalability, and business outcomes all matter at the same time. My work spans storefronts, admin systems, mobile experiences, analytics layers, and automation-heavy workflows.
profile.approach: I care about building high-performance, user-centric solutions with a strong focus on reliability, scalability, and seamless experience. I like turning practical business needs into systems that feel polished and easy to operate.

education.detail: Completed senior secondary schooling in Delhi, India.

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Computer Science
education.detail: Computer Science degree focused on software engineering, system design, and scalable digital product development.

contact.heading: Need a full stack developer for products, storefronts, dashboards, or internal systems?
contact.intro: I work across frontend, backend, mobile, analytics, and workflow automation with a focus on real business outcomes and scalable delivery.

projects.title: MIRAY E-commerce Platform
projects.description: A scalable D2C e-commerce system with admin dashboards, analytics, refund workflows, and operational automation.
projects.summary: Built and managed a full stack commerce platform covering storefront, backend, operations, and admin control surfaces.
projects.problem: The business needed a scalable commerce system that could handle storefront growth, admin control, refunds, and operational complexity without tool sprawl.
projects.solution: Developed an end-to-end platform with business-focused dashboards, fulfillment logic, analytics visibility, and automation-friendly workflows.
impact: Created a scalable D2C commerce foundation
Supported business process optimization through integrated systems

projects.title: HireHead AI
projects.description: An AI recruitment platform for interview workflows and resume analysis using machine learning and NLP.
projects.summary: Built an AI-powered hiring product focused on candidate screening and interview intelligence.
projects.problem: Recruitment workflows often require repetitive resume screening and unstructured interview evaluation.
projects.solution: Developed a platform that supports AI-powered interview assistance and resume analysis through ML and NLP workflows.
impact: Added intelligence to candidate evaluation workflows

projects.title: Foton Power
projects.description: A photography e-commerce platform with responsive UI, analytics integration, and scalable frontend architecture.
projects.summary: Built a modern commerce experience with product presentation, performance-aware frontend patterns, and analytics support.
projects.problem: The product needed a modern e-commerce interface that could balance strong presentation with scalable technical structure.
projects.solution: Developed a responsive commerce platform with analytics integration and a frontend architecture designed for scale.

blogs.title: Building Business-Focused Systems That Actually Scale
blogs.excerpt: Why scalable architecture only matters when it also makes day-to-day business workflows easier.

content.heading: Scalability Is a Business Problem First
content.body: Scalable software is not only about handling more traffic. It is about making sure product, operations, and business workflows remain stable as the company grows.

content.heading: Good Systems Reduce Friction
content.body: The best backend and admin systems create fewer repeated tasks, fewer hidden dependencies, and clearer operational visibility.

blogs.title: Why Admin Dashboards Need Product Thinking
blogs.excerpt: Internal tools deserve the same clarity, responsiveness, and UX discipline as customer-facing products.

content.heading: Internal Tools Still Shape Performance
content.body: If a team spends hours inside a dashboard, that interface has a direct impact on output, error rate, and operational speed.

content.heading: Good UX Helps Teams Move Faster
content.body: Well-designed admin systems reduce handoffs, improve confidence, and make business workflows easier to manage.

blogs.title: Automation Without Chaos
blogs.excerpt: Automation works best when it removes repetitive effort without making systems harder to understand.

content.heading: The Goal Is Reliability
content.body: Automation should make processes more dependable, not more fragile. Clear inputs, traceable outputs, and simple monitoring matter.

content.heading: Business Workflows Need Visibility
content.body: Teams trust automation more when they can see what happened, what changed, and what still needs human judgment.
